title: What Scripture actually says about humility (it's not weakness)
caption:
Humility isn't weakness.

The world calls it that because the world can't dominate a humble man. He doesn't play by those rules.

Scripture uses a different word: meek. And the meek don't think less of themselves — they just think of themselves less. That's the whole shift.

David on his face before God, unmoved before Goliath. Jesus weeping with Mary, then braiding a whip and clearing the temple. Same men. Same character. Strength under authority — not strength abandoned.

The proud man can't be corrected. Can't take instruction. Can't see himself as he actually is. He's locked inside his own version of the story, and nothing true gets in.

"Pride goes before destruction, and a haughty spirit before a fall." — Proverbs 16:18.

The governed man gets on his knees because he knows who holds him. And that's exactly what makes him impossible to move.

—

Comment "humble" and I'll send you the link to ETHOS.
